nodes.label: AI as collaborative writing partner
nodes.label_negative: AI as autonomous writer replacing human control

nodes.label_negative: AI as unable to capture personal nuance
nodes.grounding_span: help me find the right words

nodes.label: AI as future autonomous task handler
nodes.label_negative: AI as currently unreliable for independent tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I would love for an AI tool to go over the list, flag likely errors for review, and then once I approve them automatically pull up and email to the agent, draft and send it

nodes.label: AI as productivity enhancer for routine tasks
nodes.grounding_span: It spends more time telling me that it can't do things I know it should be able to do or telling me that it did do something when it didn't, then actually doing what I need

nodes.label: AI as skill requiring prompt engineering
nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to job security
nodes.grounding_span: getting them to deliver something very specific when it comes to tone, content, and accuracy is a skill in itself

nodes.label: AI as tool to free humans for creative work
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for human workers
nodes.grounding_span: AI should not replace humans in the work force, but instead should be a tool to help reduce time consuming low importance tasks to free them up for higher stakes and more creative endeavors

nodes.label: Personal expression and authenticity
nodes.grounding_span: the nuance of my tone right without me there for final edits

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: save hours of time for me

nodes.label: Professional competence and career resilience
nodes.grounding_span: I want to continue to learn about AI so when a role does transition I can be the guy who is responsible for going into the backend and adjusting the settings and prompts and doing QA work
